title: Sigma

headings.text: 2- Core Components of a Sigma Rule

overview: Sigma is an open-source, generic signature format for log-based detection. It uses YAML to define structured rules, enabling security analysts to detect threats across various SIEMs. Sigma rules specify log sources, conditions, and metadata, making them adaptable to different query languages. Essentially, Sigma standardizes log analysis, much like Snort does

study.overview: Sigma is an open-source, generic signature format for log-based detection. It uses YAML to define structured rules, enabling security analysts to detect threats across various SIEMs. Sigma rules specify log sources, conditions, and metadata, making them adaptable to different query languages. Essentially, Sigma standardizes log analysis, much like Snort does
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: 2- Core Components of a Sigma Rule
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
